author: Ahmed Mukarram
title: Bara Imam, Hissa Awwal
gloss: An account of the life of Ali, the Fourth Caliph of Islam.
notes: verso index: '24.3 Barat Imam I'. Life of Ali, Part I.

author: Aulad Haidar
title: Siraj-ul-Mubin fi Tarikh-i-Amir-ul-Mominin
gloss: A comprehensive account of the life of Ali, the Fourth Caliph of Islam.
notes: Second life-of-Ali this quarter (Shia hagiography); 496 pp. Dual date: 1327 Hijri.

title: Khud Amoz Shakhs
gloss: A short account of the lives of eighteen famous inventors and learned men of Europe who rose from poverty to power.
notes: 'Self-Made Men' — 18 European inventors as self-help exemplars; the Samuel-Smiles improvement genre in Urdu.

author: Muhammad Din, M., Fauq
title: Halat-i-Tan Sen
gloss: A brief account of the life of Tan Sen, the greatest musician of India. He was a courtier of Akbar and died in 1595 A.D.
notes: Biography of Tansen, Akbar's legendary court musician, by Muhammad Din Fauq — a music-history entry; connects to the music-colonization thread (Tansen as the classical-canon origin figure) and to Fauq's Q4 exam-guide output.

author: Raghbir Singh, B.
title: Bikarmajit-A'zam
gloss: A brief outline of the life of Vikarmaditya the Great, the famous Raja of ancient India, born in 89 B.C.
publisher: Hakim Ram Kishen, Shah Alami Gate
notes: Vikramaditya biography, 8th edition; a Rajput/Hindu-glory subject by a Sikh author. Hakim Ram Kishen publisher (cf. his prolific Q4 output).

author: Raghbir Singh, B.
title: Rana Sanga
gloss: An account of the life of Rana Sanga, a ruler of Mewar, who fought against Babar, the founder of the Mughal Empire in India.
notes: Author dash-continuation of s7. Rana Sanga vs Babur — Rajput resistance to the Mughals as biographical subject (a Rajput-heroism cluster, s7-10).

author: Raj Narayan
title: Bapa Rawal
gloss: Or a short sketch of the life of Bapa Rawal, the progenitor of the present family of the Ranas of Mewar.
notes: Bappa Rawal, founder of the Mewar dynasty — Rajput-genealogy biography.

title: Chanchal Kumari
gloss: The story of Chanchal Kumari, daughter of the Rana of Rupnagar in Rajputana, whose hand Emperor Aurangzeb is said to have asked in marriage, but as the request was not granted, the Emperor unsuccessfully attacked Rupnagar.
notes: Rajput-honour-vs-Aurangzeb tale (the plot of Bankim's later-famous Rajsimha) — the Rajput-resistance biographical cluster (s7-10) all from the Dipak Rajput Press, whose very name signals the theme.
